title: Insert & Append
intro: There are many ways into Insert mode: `i` before the cursor, `a` after it, `A` at the end of the line, `I` at the first non-blank. `o` opens a new line below, `O` above. Pick the one that saves motions.
summary: Enter Insert mode exactly where you need it.

exercises.instruction: Add a semicolon at the end of the line: press `A`, type `;`, then `Esc`.
hints: `A` jumps to the end of the line and enters Insert mode — no need to move first.

exercises.instruction: Add `const ` at the very start of the line: press `I`, type `const ` (with a trailing space), then `Esc`.
hints: `I` jumps to the first non-blank character and enters Insert mode.

exercises.instruction: Open a new line below and add `return;`: press `o`, type `return;`, then `Esc`.
hints: `o` opens a fresh line below the cursor and enters Insert mode.

exercises.instruction: Open a line ABOVE and add a comment: press `O`, type `// header`, then `Esc`.
hints: `O` (Shift+o) opens a line above the cursor.

exercises.instruction: Add a semicolon to BOTH lines: `A;` `Esc` on the first, `j`, then `A;` `Esc` again.
hints: `A` jumps to the end of the line and enters Insert mode in one keystroke.

exercises.instruction: Add a comment above with `O`, type `// start`, press `Esc` — then `j` down and append a `;` with `A;` `Esc`.
hints: `O` opens a line above; after `Esc`, `j` returns to the code line.
